description: A person or institution is 'lothoni' when a stated relation requires them to answer for a matter and undergo review. The keeper of a shared store may be accountable for its records without having caused a missing item. With 'thonai' (responsible), someone willingly takes the matter up; 'naseru' (obligation) is a requirement that may be imposed or disputed.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l', let air pass along its sides, and round the lips into true-mid back 'o'. Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', pass air along it, and keep the lips rounded for stressed true-mid back 'o'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: 'lothoni' can modify a role. Predicatively, it follows the bearer and precedes 'nai' (be). The surrounding account identifies the review relation and the matter to be answered for. Accountability is its noun reading. When exact legal liability matters, its source term keeps that force.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A shared store needs records that outlast goodwill and memory; 'lothoni' identifies who must answer when the account is challenged.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Requiring an account without presuming guilt leaves review open to evidence and repair rather than automatic punishment.

semantic_domains.community: answerability for a shared matter under a review relationship
semantic_domains.ethics: review that keeps answerability distinct from presumed blame
